tools.name: list_databases
tools.description:
List all configured database connections and their status.

Returns JSON with connection names, types, status, and masked URLs.

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description:
List all tables in a database.

Args:
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: get_table_info
tools.description:
Get detailed information about a table: columns, types, row count.

Args:
    table: Table name.
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: get_schema
tools.description:
Get the full database schema (CREATE TABLE statements for all tables).

Args:
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Execute a read-only SQL query (SELECT, SHOW, DESCRIBE, EXPLAIN) and return results.

Args:
    sql: SQL query to execute.
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    max_rows: Maximum number of rows to return (default: 100).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: execute
tools.description:
Execute a write SQL statement (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). Only works if writes are enabled.

Args:
    sql: SQL statement to execute.
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: sample_rows
tools.description:
Get a sample of rows from a table to understand its data.

Args:
    table: Table name.
    limit: Number of rows to sample (default: 5, max: 20).
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: search_tables
tools.description:
Search for tables or columns matching a keyword.

Args:
    keyword: Keyword to search for in table and column names.
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: schema_diff
tools.description:
Compare schemas between two database connections.

Args:
    source_connection: Source connection name.
    target_connection: Target connection name.
    table_name: Optional table name to diff. If empty, diff all tables.

tools.name: check_health
tools.description:
Check database health: latency, table count, row count, largest tables.

Args:
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: generate_er_diagram
tools.description:
Generate an ER (Entity-Relationship) diagram in Mermaid format.

Args:
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").
    format: Output format. Currently only 'mermaid' is supported.

tools.name: explain_query
tools.description:
Explain the execution plan for a SELECT query.

Args:
    query: SQL SELECT or WITH...SELECT statement to explain.
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

tools.name: diagnose_connection
tools.description:
Diagnose a database connection with detailed status and troubleshooting hints.

Args:
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").

resources.name: resource_mergewall_audit_schema
resources.description: Return the suggested Mergewall audit table schema.

findings.title: Scope Creep — 'get_schema' is named read-only but description implies write access
findings.description: Tool 'get_schema' has a read-only prefix but its description mentions write operations ('CREATE'), creating a scope mismatch that callers and LLMs may be unable to detect.
findings.evidence:
Get the full database schema (CREATE TABLE statements for all tables).

Args:
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").
findings.remediation: Separate read and write operations into distinct tools. Ensure tool names accurately reflect their full capabilities.

findings.title: Parameter 'database' in 'list_tables' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'database'.

findings.title: Parameter 'database' in 'get_table_info' has no type constraint
findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'database'.

findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'database'.

findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'database'.

findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.
findings.remediation: Add an explicit type to parameter 'database'.

findings.description: Untyped parameters accept any value, making input validation harder to enforce.

findings.title: 'check_health' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: 'generate_er_diagram' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: 'diagnose_connection' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'query' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'query' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence:
Execute a read-only SQL query (SELECT, SHOW, DESCRIBE, EXPLAIN) and return results.

Args:
    sql: SQL query to execute.
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    max_rows: Maximum number of rows to return (default: 100).
    connection_name: Name of the database conn
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'execute' (scope-restricted)
findings.description: Tool 'execute' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'execute' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'execute' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence:
Execute a write SQL statement (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). Only works if writes are enabled.

Args:
    sql: SQL statement to execute.
    database: Name of the database within the connection (optional).
    connection_name: Name of the database connection (default: "default").
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: 'list_tables' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 12 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Protocol crash — JSON-RPC batch array caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a JSON-RPC 2.0 batch (5 requests as an array) caused the server to crash or disconnect. MCP does not define batch semantics; servers must reject arrays gracefully.
findings.evidence: Server disconnected after batch. Pre-crash response: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":62,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Error: Write operations are dis
findings.remediation: Validate input type at the JSON-RPC layer: if the parsed value is a list, return -32600 (Invalid Request) immediately without further processing.

findings.title: Protocol crash — oversized method name caused transport failure
findings.description: The server failed to respond to a request with an 8192-byte method name. A robust server should return a JSON-RPC error, not disconnect or crash.
findings.remediation: Add input length validation at the JSON-RPC parsing layer. Return -32700 (parse error) or -32600 (invalid request) for malformed inputs.

findings.title: Protocol crash — deeply nested params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request with 50-level nested params caused the server to disconnect or crash. Deep nesting can cause stack overflows or excessive memory use in recursive JSON parsers.
findings.remediation: Limit JSON object nesting depth at parse time (recommended: ≤32 levels). Return -32700 (Parse error) for inputs exceeding the depth limit.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: Server accepts array request ID — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server returned a successful result to a request with an array as the request ID. JSON-RPC 2.0 requires the id to be a string, number, or null.
findings.remediation: Validate that the request 'id' is a string, number, or null. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for array or object IDs.
